hook.text: PayPal started as cryptography for Palm Pilots. It pivoted five times before finding its product — online payments for eBay.

buildup.text: The team noticed eBay sellers were using PayPal to accept payments. This wasn't the plan — users found a use case the team missed.

discovery.text: PayPal leaned into eBay. They let sellers embed PayPal buttons in auction listings — growth exploded.

twist.text: eBay tried to kill PayPal by launching its own payment system, Billpoint. Sellers preferred PayPal and refused to switch.

climax.text: eBay bought PayPal for $1.5 billion. A product growing inside a platform became more valuable than the platform's own tool.

punchline.text: Users showed PayPal its product. PayPal was smart enough to listen.

quiz.question: How did PayPal find its product-market fit?
options: Users on eBay adopted it organically — the team followed user behavior
The team planned eBay integration from day one
PayPal was built specifically for eBay by eBay
